title: ## PRINCIPLES AND GUIDANCE ON THE SELECTION OF REPRESENTATIVE COMMODITIES FOR THE EXTRAPOLATION OF MAXIMUM RESIDUE LIMITS FOR PESTICIDES TO COMMODITY GROUPS CXG 84-2012 Adopted in 2012. Amended in 2017.

sections.section: ## PRINCIPLES AND GUIDANCE ON THE SELECTION OF REPRESENTATIVE COMMODITIES FOR THE EXTRAPOLATION OF MAXIMUM RESIDUE LIMITS FOR PESTICIDES TO COMMODITY GROUPS CXG 84-2012 Adopted in 2012. Amended in 2017.
sections.summary: Heading/title only stating the document name and adoption/amendment dates (Principles and Guidance on selection of representative commodities for extrapolation of MRLs, adopted 2012, amended 2017); not substantive text.

Defines residue extrapolation and states representative commodities should be chosen for commercial importance and similarity in morphology and residue behaviour to related commodities, with the document’s objectives to propose selection criteria, example representatives and detailed justifications.

Sets three selection principles for representative commodities—most likely to contain highest residues, major in production/consumption, and similar in morphology/growth habit/pest problems/edible portion—notes the selection assumes similar use patterns/GAP, allows alternative representatives for regional differences, and references Table 1 and supporting justification documents.

Describes the procedure: representative‑commodity proposals will accompany Classification revisions through CCPR/CAC step procedures, two separate documents (Classification and selection guidance) will be maintained, JMPR may be advised to use CAC‑adopted representatives but may use alternatives if justified case‑by‑case and must provide justification to CCPR.

sections.section: Subgroup 006C, Assorted tropical and sub-tropical, Inedible, Rough or Hairy Peel - Large
sections.summary: Subgroup listing for 006C (Assorted tropical and sub‑tropical, inedible rough or hairy peel — large) providing example commodities such as Atemoya, Pineapple, Durian, Jackfruit, Rambutan, Soursop, Sapodilla and others.

sections.section: Subgroup 006D, Assorted tropical and sub-tropical, Inedible Peel - Cactus
sections.summary: Subgroup listing for 006D (Assorted tropical and sub‑tropical, inedible peel — cactus) identifying Pitaya and Prickly pear (and Saguaro) as members.

sections.section: Subgroup 006E, Assorted tropical and sub-tropical, Inedible Peel - Vines
sections.summary: Subgroup listing for 006E (Assorted tropical and sub‑tropical, inedible peel — vines) identifying Kiwifruit and Passionfruit group members (e.g., Granadilla, Kiwifruit, Passionfruit and related species).

sections.section: Subgroup 006F, Assorted tropical and sub-tropical, Inedible Peel - Palms
sections.summary: Defines Subgroup 006F (Assorted tropical and sub-tropical, Inedible Peel - Palms) and lists included commodities such as Muriti/Palmyra Palm and members with inedible peels (FI 2026): young coconut, Guriri, Muriti, Palmyra Palm, and Salak.

Defines Subgroup 013A (Leafy greens) as including head and leaf lettuce, spinach and a detailed list of specific edible leaves and leafy vegetables (VL 2050) such as African eggplant leaves, amaranth leaves, chard, chicory leaves, corn salad, etc.
